summary: zod-pino ships credential-scanning, Discord relay upload, and postinstall agent — supply-chain attack disguised as a logging utility
details: Package is published under the name `zod-pino`, suggesting an integration between the popular `zod` validation library and the `pino` logger, but the shipped contents have no relation to either project. The tarball ships modules for scanning installer secrets and relaying data off-host: `dist/secretScan/agentStartupAudit.js` and `dist/secretScan/contentScanner.js` perform credential/secret enumeration with base64-encoded patterns; `dist/discordRelayUpload.js` performs base64 decoding and POSTs data to remote endpoints (Discord relay upload pattern); `dist/hfCredentials.js` handles HuggingFace credentials with base64-decoded blobs; `dist/relayServer.js` runs a relay server with command-execution surface (`ping` invocations alongside POST flows); `dist/secretScan/agentStartupAudit.js` makes outbound `fetch()` calls to `https://huggingface.co` from a startup-audit code path. Additionally, `scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs` runs at npm postinstall time and contains command-execution + HTTP GET primitives, meaning the agent fires on `npm install` without user interaction. The combination of postinstall-time agent activation, secret-scanning modules that walk credential locations, base64-encoded payload material, and an outbound relay server is the canonical exfiltration-and-C2 fingerprint. The package name does not match its contents, and the shipped functionality is materially harmful to any installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
